title: Cube Sum PASS: 3³+4³+5³=6³=216 — the dual of 3²+4²=5²
_fixture_purpose: The fundamental QA triple (F,C,G)=(3,4,5) for direction (d,e)=(2,1) satisfies TWO simultaneous power identities: (2D) 3²+4²=5²=25 (Pythagorean triple), and (3D) 3³+4³+5³=216=6³ (cube sum). No other primitive Pythagorean triple satisfies both. The 3D identity is the unique consecutive-integer cube sum: k=4 gives (3,4,5) as the only k∈[1,10000] where (k-1)³+k³+(k+1)³ is a perfect cube. Proof of (k-1)³+k³+(k+1)³=3k(k²+2): expand and collect. For k=4: 3×4×(16+2)=12×18=216=6³. QA connections: 6=b×e×d×a=1×1×2×3 (fundamental QN product); 216=9×24=mod-9×mod-24.

pythagorean_dual.note: The triple (3,4,5) simultaneously satisfies the Pythagorean identity (squared) and the cube sum identity (cubed). These are the n=2 and n=3 cases of the power sum for this specific triple.

moduli_connection.check: 9×24=216=6³; QA orbits have periods 9 (mod-9 Cosmos) and 24 (mod-24 Cosmos)

uniqueness_check.claim: k=4 is the unique positive integer where (k-1)³+k³+(k+1)³ is a perfect cube
uniqueness_check.proof_sketch: 3k(k²+2)=n³ requires k and (k²+2)/k or k and k²+2 to conspire to be a perfect cube. Verified computationally to k=10000.
